mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real personal consumption expenditures, proxied by retail sales RSAFS, signals expanding aggregate demand that drives corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS on 20-day forward returns is less than 0.05 over any 3-year walk-forward window, or the correlation turns negative out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production INDPRO reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate profits and stock prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO and forward 60-day returns is below 0.05 over a 5-year period, or the predictive regression R-squared is less than 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in the unemployment rate UNRATE signals tightening labor market conditions, leading to wage inflation pressures that compress profit margins and reduce equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.3 pp over a rolling 3-month period
failure_condition: In walk-forward tests, instances triggered by this condition produce positive returns more than 50% of the time, or the average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4%, a further increase of at least 25 basis points in one month signals aggressive monetary tightening that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 0.25% or more in one month
failure_condition: Conditional average 5-day return following triggers is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of the federal funds rate and consumer price index determines the real interest rate. When the real rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL annual change) rises above 2%, tight monetary policy depresses equity valuations over a 60-day horizon. The interaction of both series is necessary.
candidate_trigger: Real rate (FEDFUNDS minus annual CPIAUCSL change) > 2%
failure_condition: The real rate signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than half of the triggered instances, or the interaction term in a regression model is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The ratio of retail sales to industrial production (RSAFS/INDPRO) captures the balance between demand and supply. A rising ratio indicates demand outstripping supply, leading to inventory build and future production cuts, which is negative for stocks over a 60-day horizon. The interaction between the two series is required.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS/INDPRO ratio is above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: The ratio signal fails to consistently predict negative returns; the top decile of ratio values does not produce average negative 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a full-employment regime (UNRATE ≤ 4.5%), further job growth (PAYEMS) leads to wage inflation that pressures corporate margins, causing negative equity returns over a 20-day horizon. The mechanism depends on the joint state of UNRATE and PAYEMS.
failure_condition: In the full-employment regime, the average return following months of above-median PAYEMS growth is not negative, or the negative predictive power is weaker than in the high-unemployment regime.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing downturn, a sharp decline in housing starts HOUST signals further weakness in residential investment and consumer spending, leading to negative equity returns over a 20-day horizon. The mechanism is conditional on being in a contraction phase.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is below its 12-month MA and falling month-over-month
failure_condition: The conditional signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns more often than a random coin flip over a 2-year walk-forward period.
